title: feat(langchain): add create_agent_tool and AgentSession for nested loops and time-travel
body_text:
## Summary

Implements loop-in-loop agent patterns and session management requested in #35452.

- **`create_agent_tool`**: wraps a compiled agent graph as a `BaseTool` so an outer agent can delegate subtasks to an inner agent that runs its own complete tool-calling loop — the "loop in loop" pattern.
- **`AgentSession`**: convenience wrapper for interrupt/resume workflows and checkpoint-based time-travel (branching from any historical state into a new session).

Both APIs are marked as experimental and exported from `langchain.agents`.

### Areas requiring careful review

- `_AgentTool._run` / `_arun` — runtime import of `CallbackManagerForToolRun` outside `TYPE_CHECKING` is intentional (pydantic resolves the `_run` signature at runtime).
- `AgentSession.resume()` — uses `None` for node-level interrupts vs `Command(resume=value)` for `interrupt()` calls; this distinction matters.
- `AgentSession.branch()` — copies state via `update_state` to a new thread; does not deep-copy message objects.

## Test plan

- [x] 6 unit tests for `create_agent_tool` (basic, nested loop-in-loop, async, thread prefix, exports)
- [x] 19 unit tests for `AgentSession` (run with all input types, interrupt/resume, state inspection, history, branching, preserving original state, async variants, exports)
- [x] All 194 existing agent tests pass (zero regressions)

> This contribution was developed with AI agent assistance.

Closes #35452

entities.name: Create Agent Tool
entities.description: A function that wraps a compiled agent graph as a BaseTool to enable loop-in-loop agent patterns.

entities.name: Agent Session
entities.description: A convenience wrapper for managing agent state, supporting interrupt/resume workflows and checkpoint-based time-travel.

entities.name: Loop-in-Loop Agent Patterns
entities.description: An architectural pattern where an outer agent delegates tasks to an inner agent, which then runs its own complete tool-calling loop.

entities.name: Session Management
entities.description: The capability to control and maintain the state of an agent's execution, including pausing, resuming, and branching.

entities.name: BaseTool
entities.description: An interface or base class for tools that agents can utilize.

entities.name: Compiled Agent Graph
entities.description: A pre-processed or optimized representation of an agent's execution flow.

entities.name: Outer Agent
entities.description: An agent designed to delegate subtasks to other agents.

entities.name: Inner Agent
entities.description: An agent that executes a specific subtask, often within its own tool-calling loop, as delegated by an outer agent.

entities.name: Tool-Calling Loop
entities.description: A process where an agent repeatedly calls tools to achieve a goal.

entities.name: Interrupt/Resume Workflows
entities.description: The ability to pause an agent's execution and later restart it from the point of interruption.

entities.name: Checkpoint-Based Time-Travel
entities.description: The ability to revert an agent's state to a previous checkpoint and branch into a new execution path.

entities.name: Langchain.agents
entities.description: The module within Langchain where the new agent APIs are exposed.

entities.name: _AgentTool._run
entities.description: A method within the internal `_AgentTool` class, whose signature resolution is handled by Pydantic.

entities.name: _arun
entities.description: An asynchronous method within the internal `_AgentTool` class, whose signature resolution is handled by Pydantic.

entities.name: CallbackManagerForToolRun
entities.description: A class related to managing callbacks during tool execution, intentionally imported at runtime.

entities.name: Pydantic
entities.description: A data validation and settings management library used for resolving method signatures at runtime.

entities.name: AgentSession.resume()
entities.description: A method of `AgentSession` used to resume agent execution.

entities.name: Command(resume=value)
entities.description: A specific command object used for `interrupt()` calls within `AgentSession`.

entities.name: AgentSession.branch()
entities.description: A method of `AgentSession` for creating a new session by copying state from an existing one.

entities.name: Update State
entities.description: An operation used to copy the state of an agent session.

entities.name: Message Objects
entities.description: Data structures representing messages within an agent's state.
